slides.title: You can't *outrun your fork*.
slides.sub: Exercise is doing less for your waistline than you think. The math is brutal once you see it.

slides.title: Workouts burn less than you hope
slides.body: A hard hour might burn *300–500 calories* — undone by one muffin in ninety seconds. Out-eating a workout is effortless.

slides.title: Most of your burn is living
slides.body: Exercise is a *small slice of daily calories* — breathing, organs and basic movement run the rest. You can't train that slice big enough.

slides.title: The watch lies upward
slides.body: Machines and trackers *overcount calories burned*, often by a lot. Then you 'earn' a treat and erase the session.

slides.title: Hard training makes you hungry
slides.body: Big sessions can leave you *hungrier and more sedentary* the rest of the day. Your body quietly claws the calories back.

slides.title: Diet for fat, train for muscle
slides.body: Lift and move for *health, muscle and mood* — build the calorie deficit in the kitchen. That's where the waistline actually changes.

slides.title: Win it *at the table*.
slides.body: Vibe Health makes the food side easy — snap meals to see intake, and watch your weight trend — so the deficit is real, not guessed.
slides.tag: fat loss is built in the kitchen

caption:
You really can't outrun your fork. A hard hour burns maybe 300–500 calories — one muffin erases it. 🍩

Exercise is a small slice of your daily burn, and trackers overcount it. Train for muscle and mood; build the deficit with food. 🔖

Vibe Health makes the food side simple — snap meals, watch the trend → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why You Can't Outrun a Bad Diet
